mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or pinned near zero, equity discount rates stay suppressed and 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive because cheaper financing supports valuations and risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.05% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with FEDFUNDS below 1%, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals labor-market deterioration that lowers expected earnings and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 14.7% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative median 20-day returns, or if the sign of the effect turns positive in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent upturn in RSAFS and HOUST marks demand acceleration in consumption and housing, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST are both available in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone, or if positive 60-day returns do not dominate when both are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and inventory pressure, which should reduce forward earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is 92.5919 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO momentum fails to align with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive during a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 over the prior quarter signals aggregate demand weakness that compresses earnings estimates and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is reported with high revision risk
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth is not followed by below-zero 60-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the effect disappears after revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated and FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates are deeply negative, which pushes investors toward equities and generates positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays below CPI inflation
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is low revision risk and FEDFUNDS is 0.05%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction does not outperform a single-series rule, or if triggered periods do not show positive median 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS count after a labor-market shock signals payroll stabilization and improving income flows, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS improvement does not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns in triggered windows, or if the effect is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand retrenchment across retail spending and housing, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not predict negative 20-day returns, or if the combined signal fails to improve on each series alone.
